phase_1.audio: Audio environment is distracting with avg dB of 62.45, exceeding the 60 dB threshold. All 4 confidence-filtered events are labeled 'busy' with 3 Speech and 1 Ambient AI labels, indicating a noisy, conversation-heavy environment unsuitable for focused study.
phase_1.vitals: Heart rate averages 89.93 bpm, elevated above the calm focus range of 60-85 bpm, suggesting stress or physical activity. Steps average 29.9 per sample and distance 26.09m per sample, indicating moderate continuous movement rather than seated study behavior.
phase_1.gps_motion: GPS shows drastic location instability with 1797.92m displacement and 3506.35m total travel distance, far exceeding stable study thresholds. Motion context shows 3 walking vs only 2 stationary periods, confirming active movement throughout the session.

phase_2.correlations: All modalities converge on a pattern of active mobility rather than concentrated study. The noisy 'busy' audio environment correlates with walking motion contexts and substantial GPS travel. Elevated heart rate aligns with physical movement (high steps/distance) and potentially stressful busy environments. The combination of speech-heavy audio, continuous walking, and kilometer-scale location changes indicates the user was likely commuting, running errands, or moving between locations rather than studying.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: This session exhibits extremely poor concentration conditions across all measured dimensions. The user was in a mobile, noisy, physically active state with no evidence of sustained stationary study behavior. Environmental noise, physiological activation, and extensive location changes all contradict focused academic work.

phase_3.recommendations: 1) Establish a dedicated, stationary study location to eliminate travel-related distractions and reduce the 3.5km of movement observed. 2) Choose quiet environments with dB levels below 45 to avoid the busy, speech-heavy audio conditions (62.45 dB) that disrupted this session. 3) Schedule study sessions separately from active/commuting periods to allow heart rate to settle into the calm 60-85 bpm range and minimize walking interruptions.

reason: The session shows severe concentration impairment across all modalities: distracting audio environment (62.45 dB with all 'busy' labels and speech), elevated heart rate (89.93 bpm), and extensive mobility (1798m displacement, 3506m travel, predominantly walking). These metrics indicate active commuting or errands rather than any meaningful study activity.
